name: jsonrpcserver
documentation: ![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/jsonrpcserver.svg) ![Code Quality](https://github.com/explodinglabs/jsonrpcserver/actions/workflows/code-quality.yml/badge.svg) ![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/explodinglabs/jsonrpcserver/badge.svg?branch=main) ![Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dw/jsonrpcserver) Process incoming JSON-RPC requests in Python. ```python from jsonrpcserver import method, serve, Success @method def ping(): return Success("pong") if __name__ == "__main__": serve() ``` Or use `dispatch` instead of `serve`: ```python response = dispatch('{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "ping", "id": 1}') # => '{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "result": "pong", "id": 1}' ``` [Watch a video on how to use it.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3_BMmgJaFHQ) Full documentation is at [jsonrpcserver.com](https://www.jsonrpcserver.com/). See also: [jsonrpcclient](https://github.com/explodinglabs/jsonrpcclient)

modules.name: setup
documentation: setup.py

modules.name: jsonrpcserver.async_main
documentation: Async version of main.py. The public async functions.

modules.name: jsonrpcserver.request
documentation:
A simple namedtuple to hold a request.

After parsing the request string, we put the requests (which are dicts) into these
Request namedtuples because they're nicer to work with.

class_variables.name: jsonrpcserver.request.Request.id
documentation: Use NOID for a Notification.

modules.name: jsonrpcserver.main
documentation:
The public functions.

These three public functions all perform the same function of dispatching a JSON-RPC
request, but they each give a different return value.

- dispatch_to_responses: Returns Response(s) (or None for notifications).
- dispatch_to_serializable: Returns a Python dict or list of dicts (or None for
  notifications).
- dispatch_to_json/dispatch: Returns a JSON-RPC response string (or an empty string for
  notifications).

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.main.dispatch_to_response
documentation:
Takes a JSON-RPC request string and dispatches it to method(s), giving Response
namedtuple(s) or None.

This is a public wrapper around dispatch_to_response_pure, adding globals and
default values to be nicer for end users.

Args:
    request: The JSON-RPC request string.
    methods: Dictionary of methods that can be called - mapping of function names to
        functions. If not passed, uses the internal global_methods dict which is
        populated with the @method decorator.
    context: If given, will be passed as the first argument to methods.
    deserializer: Function that deserializes the request string.
    validator: Function that validates the JSON-RPC request. The function should
        raise an exception if the request is invalid. To disable validation, pass
        lambda _: None.
    post_process: Function that will be applied to Responses.

Returns:
    A Response, list of Responses or None.

Examples:
   >>> dispatch('{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "ping", "id": 1}')
   '{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "result": "pong", "id": 1}'

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.main.dispatch_to_serializable
documentation:
Takes a JSON-RPC request string and dispatches it to method(s), giving responses
as dicts (or None).

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.main.dispatch_to_json
documentation:
Takes a JSON-RPC request string and dispatches it to method(s), giving a JSON-RPC
response string.

This is the main public method, it goes through the entire JSON-RPC process - it's a
function from JSON-RPC request string to JSON-RPC response string.

Args:
    serializer: A function to serialize a Python object to json.
    The rest: Passed through to dispatch_to_serializable.

modules.name: jsonrpcserver.exceptions
documentation:
A JsonRpcError exception can be raised from inside a method, as an alternative way to
return an error response. See https://github.com/explodinglabs/jsonrpcserver/discussions/158

modules.name: jsonrpcserver.sentinels
documentation:
Sentinels - these are used to indicate no data is present.

We can't use None, because None may be a valid piece of data.

modules.name: jsonrpcserver
documentation: Use __all__ so mypy considers these re-exported.

modules.name: jsonrpcserver.codes
documentation: JSONRPC error codes from http://www.jsonrpc.org/specification#error_object

modules.name: jsonrpcserver.methods
documentation:
A method is a Python function that can be called by a JSON-RPC request.

They're held in a dict, a mapping of function names to functions.

The @method decorator adds a method to jsonrpcserver's internal global_methods dict.
Alternatively pass your own dictionary of methods to `dispatch` with the methods param.

    >>> dispatch(request)  # Uses the internal collection of funcs added with @method
    >>> dispatch(request, methods={"ping": lambda: "pong"})  # Custom collection

Methods can take either positional or named arguments, but not both. This is a
limitation of JSON-RPC.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.methods.method
documentation:
A decorator to add a function into jsonrpcserver's internal global_methods dict.
The global_methods dict will be used by default unless a methods argument is passed
to `dispatch`.

Functions can be renamed by passing a name argument:

    @method(name=bar)
    def foo():
        ...

modules.name: jsonrpcserver.async_dispatcher
documentation: Async version of dispatcher.py

modules.name: jsonrpcserver.result
documentation:
Result data types - the results of calling a method.

Results are the JSON-RPC response objects
(https://www.jsonrpc.org/specification#response_object), minus the "jsonrpc" and "id"
parts - the library takes care of these parts for you.

The public functions are Success, Error and InvalidParams.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.result.MethodNotFoundResult
documentation: Helpers

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.result.Success
documentation: Helpers (the public functions)

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.result.InvalidParams
documentation:
InvalidParams is a shortcut to save you from having to pass the Invalid Params
JSON-RPC code to Error.

class_variables.name: jsonrpcserver.result.ErrorResult.data
documentation: The spec says this value may be omitted

modules.name: jsonrpcserver.response
documentation:
The response data types.

https://www.jsonrpc.org/specification#response_object

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.response.ParseErrorResponse
documentation:
From the spec: "This (id) member is REQUIRED. It MUST be the same as the value of
the id member in the Request Object.  If there was an error in detecting the id in
the Request object (e.g. Parse error/Invalid Request), it MUST be Null."

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.response.InvalidRequestResponse
documentation:
From the spec: "This (id) member is REQUIRED. It MUST be the same as the value of
the id member in the Request Object.  If there was an error in detecting the id in
the Request object (e.g. Parse error/Invalid Request), it MUST be Null."

classes.name: jsonrpcserver.response.SuccessResponse
documentation:
It would be nice to subclass Success here, adding only id. But it's not possible to
easily subclass NamedTuples in Python 3.6. (I believe it can be done in 3.8.)

classes.name: jsonrpcserver.response.ErrorResponse
documentation:
It would be nice to subclass Error here, adding only id. But it's not possible to
easily subclass NamedTuples in Python 3.6. (I believe it can be done in 3.8.)

modules.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher
documentation:
Dispatcher - does the hard work of this library: parses, validates and dispatches
requests, providing responses.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.extract_list
documentation:
This is the inverse of make_list. Here we extract a response back out of the list
if it wasn't a batch request originally. Also applies a JSON-RPC rule: we do not
respond to batches of notifications.

Args:
    is_batch: True if the original request was a batch.
    responses: Iterable of responses.

Returns: A single response, a batch of responses, or None (returns None to a
    notification or batch of notifications, to indicate we should not respond).

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.to_response
documentation:
Maps a Request plus a Result to a Response. A Response is just a Result plus the
id from the original Request.

Raises: AssertionError if the request is a notification. Notifications can't be
    responded to. If a notification is given and AssertionError is raised, we should
    respond with Server Error, because notifications should have been removed by
    this stage.

Returns: A Response.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.extract_args
documentation:
Extracts the positional arguments from the request.

If a context object is given, it's added as the first argument.

Returns: A list containing the positional arguments.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.extract_kwargs
documentation:
Extracts the keyword arguments from the reqeust.

Returns: A dict containing the keyword arguments.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.validate_result
documentation:
Validate the return value from a method.

Raises an AssertionError if the result returned from a method is invalid.

Returns: None

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.call
documentation:
Call the method.

Handles any exceptions raised in the method, being sure to return an Error response.

Returns: A Result.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.validate_args
documentation:
Ensure the method can be called with the arguments given.

Returns: Either the function to be called, or an Invalid Params error result.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.get_method
documentation:
Get the requested method from the methods dict.

Returns: Either the function to be called, or a Method Not Found result.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.dispatch_request
documentation:
Get the method, validates the arguments and calls the method.

Returns: A tuple containing the Result of the method, along with the original
    Request. We need the ids from the original request to remove notifications
    before responding, and  create a Response.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.create_request
documentation: Create a Request namedtuple from a dict.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.not_notification
documentation:
True if the request was not a notification.

Used to filter out notifications from the list of responses.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.dispatch_deserialized
documentation:
This is simply continuing the pipeline from dispatch_to_response_pure. It exists
only to be an abstraction, otherwise that function is doing too much. It continues
on from the request string having been parsed and validated.

Returns: A Response, a list of Responses, or None. If post_process is passed, it's
    applied to the Response(s).

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.validate_request
documentation:
Validate the request against a JSON-RPC schema.

Ensures the parsed request is valid JSON-RPC.

Returns: Either the same request passed in or an Invalid request response.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.deserialize_request
documentation:
Parse the JSON request string.

Returns: Either the deserialized request or a "Parse Error" response.

functions.name: jsonrpcserver.dispatcher.dispatch_to_response_pure
documentation:
A function from JSON-RPC request string to Response namedtuple(s), (yet to be
serialized to json).

Returns: A single Response, a list of Responses, or None. None is given for
    notifications or batches of notifications, to indicate that we should not
    respond.
